steps.title: Identify the population vs. the sample
steps.content: The city council wanted to assess the opinions of all city residents, but the survey was conducted only on residents who own dogs. These are not the same population. By design, the survey excluded all residents who do not own dogs.

steps.title: Recognize why the sample is biased
steps.content: Dog owners have a natural interest in a dog park—they own dogs. This group cannot represent the opinions of the entire city, which includes people who don't own dogs and may have different views. When a sample doesn't reflect the composition of the target population, it is biased and not representative of the entire group. That matches choice D — D is correct.
